news_title: Biodexa Moves Into Phase 3 With eRapa For FAP With First Patients Enrolled
summary: Biodexa Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:BDRX)has initiated its Phase 3 trial for eRapa, a proprietary encapsulated rapamycin treatment for familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP). The company received an additional$3 million grantfrom the Cancer Prevention & Research Institute of Texas, bringing total non-dilutive funding to$20 millionfor the Phase 3 trial.The Phase 2 trial showed promising results with a29% median decrease in polyp burdenand89% non-progression rateat 12 months in cohort 2. The Phase 3 study will involve168 patientsacross approximately 30 clinical sites in the U.S. and Europe. With Fast Track status and Orphan Drug designation, Biodexa is positioned to be first-to-market in an estimated$7.3 billionaddressable market with no current therapeutic options.
